summary: High‑octane Hampton show with scorching China>Rider and fiery Alabama
blurb: The Dead launch the night with a blistering Alabama Getaway → Greatest Story opener and keep the momentum rolling through a searing China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent delivers an unforgettable Superstitious/Bottom solo, and the second‑set jams on The Other One and Wharf Rat are tight and adventurous.
review: On March 21, 1985 the Grateful Dead turned the Hampton Coliseum into a high‑energy crucible. From the first note of “Alabama Getaway” through a seamless transition into “Greatest Story Ever Told,” the band was locked in, with Jerry’s edgy vocal on the opener giving way to his signature face‑melting solos – especially the soaring lead on “China Cat Sunflower.” Brent Mydland added texture and drama, highlighted by an unreal solo during the “I Ain’t Superstitous” → “Down In The Bottom” sequence. Phil Lesh kept a solid, heady bass foundation while Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart’s drum interplay drove the improvisational peaks on “Playing In The Band,” “The Other One,” and “Wharf Rat.” Bob Weir stepped forward vocally when Garcia dipped, delivering strong, quirky moments that helped balance the set. Despite a few vocal slips and minor tape edits in some audience recordings, the performance is consistently praised across sources as one of the band’s tighter mid‑’80s outings.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of eight analyses are positive, highlighting standout jams and strong playing; two mixed notes add nuance but no pervasive criticism, only a single negative comment about one off‑key vocal. This consistent praise warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Fair‑quality audience tape that captures the show’s high energy and key highlights with decent clarity, making it the most complete and listenable source.

key_highlights: Explosive China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Fiery Alabama Getaway opening feeding into Greatest Story Ever Told
Powerful jams on The Other One, Wharf Rat, and Playing In The Band
Brent’s standout Superstitious/Bottom solo with slide work

band_performance.Jerry: Edgy vocal on the opener gives way to fiery, face‑melting guitar solos throughout, especially on China and Alabama; mixed remarks on stage presence but overall praised for his lead work.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, heady bass foundation that underpins the improvisations, staying steady despite pre‑show drama.
band_performance.Bob: Steps up with strong vocals and quirky moments, keeping the energy high when Garcia pulls back.
band_performance.Brent: Shines with slide textures and an unforgettable solo on Superstitious/Bottom; keyboards are described as 'unreal' during the China > Rider segment.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming praised for tight interplay with Bill, adding drive to the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drum work that meshes well with Mickey; despite some tape cuts in audience recordings, his performance remains a highlight.
